title: AmendmentProposal
description: Schema for a constitutional amendment proposal produced by the Constitution Steward layer. Proposes changes to prompts, policies, schemas, docs, or workflows. Cannot self-approve — requires human review gate. Aligned with docs/ai-native/amendment-proposal-schema.md.

proposalId.description: Machine-readable idempotency marker (e.g. amend-1075-schema, amend-health-gate). Used to deduplicate proposals across cycles.

targetDomain.description: Constitutional domain being amended. prompt = agent role instructions, policy = governance rules, schema = JSON schema definitions, doc = documentation, workflow = scripts/automation, gate = review/merge gates.

targetPath.description: File path or identifier of the artifact being amended (e.g. schemas/task-v2.schema.json, docs/ai-native/main-health-policy.md).

currentBehavior.description: What the artifact does today. Must cite specific fields, rules, or sections — not vague summaries. Satisfies the Reality law.

proposedChange.description: Precise description of the change. Must specify what is added, removed, or modified. Diff-level specificity preferred.

justification.description: Why the change is needed. Must reference evidence: gap analysis, contradiction, missing coverage, or external requirement. Satisfies the Selection law.

factsVerified.description: Specific facts the steward verified before proposing (e.g. 'task-v2.schema.json has no amendment lifecycle field', 'health-state allows workerClass=all on red'). Each must be checkable.

assumptions.description: Unverified assumptions that should be confirmed during review.

conflictsWith.description: Existing rules or schemas this amendment may conflict with.

realityCheck.description: Evidence grounding for the proposal. Required by the Reality law — proposals without verified facts are invalid.

risk.description: Amendment risk level. low = docs/typos, medium = schema/policy changes, high = gate/weakening safety boundaries. High-risk amendments require constitution-steward-reviewer AND ai-governance-reviewer.

preventsSelfApproval.description: Must be true. The steward cannot approve its own proposals — this field is a structural reminder.

requiresHumanGate.description: Must be true. All amendments require human review before application.

escapeHatch.description: How to reject or defer this proposal without applying it. Typically 'close-issue' or 'defer-to-next-wave'.

governedRecursion.description: Governed recursion constraints. Ensures the steward does not self-expand or bypass human gates.

status.description: Lifecycle state. proposed = initial submission, under-review = assigned to reviewers, approved = human approved but not yet applied, rejected = human declined, applied = change landed, deferred = postponed to future wave.

proposedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the proposal was created.

proposedBy.description: Identity of the proposer (e.g. constitution-steward-worker, human:taoyu). Must not be the same as the approver.

reviewedBy.description: Identity of the reviewer who approved or rejected. Null until reviewed.

reviewedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the review decision was made.

rejectionReason.description: Required when status is rejected. Explains why the proposal was declined.

appliedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the approved change was applied.

relatedIssue.description: GitHub issue number that triggered or tracks this proposal.

relatedPR.description: GitHub PR number that implements this amendment, if applicable.

rollbackPlan.description: How to undo this amendment if it causes problems after application.

tags.description: Filterable tags for grouping proposals (e.g. wave32, gate-hardening, schema-gap).
